LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ALL CONTINENTS TRAVEL, INC. DBA PICASSO TRAVEL, RAINER KLEE, HUSEYIN OZYURTCU, EREN AKSOY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO AUGUST 8, 2012. THE PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE, TO DEPARTMENT 302, NO LATER THAN AUGUST 1, 2012 AT 4:00 PM. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976
POS OF OPPOSITION TO DEFTS DEMURRER TO AMENDED AND SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF TRAVELVIVA, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ALL CONTINENTS TRAVEL, INC. DBA PICASSO TRAVEL, RAINER KLEE, HUSEYIN OZYURTCU AND EREN AKSOY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. REGARDING THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PROPERLY PLED A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING THAT SOUNDS IN CONTRACT. (SEE FOLEY V. INTERACTIVE DATA CORP. (1988) 47 CAL.3D 654, 683-684.) REGARDING THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL IS PROPERLY PLED AS AN ALTERNATIVE TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. REGARDING THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, WITHOUT MORE, A BREACH OF CONTRACT CANNOT BE A PREDICATE FOR UCL LIABILITY AS A MATTER OF LAW. (PUENTES V. WELLS FARGO HOME MORTG., INC. (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 638, 645.) PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING HOW DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS WERE UNLAWFUL, UNFAIR OR FRAUDULENT IN ADDITION TO BEING A BREACH OF CONTRACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-12-2013 IN 504, PLAINTIFF TRAVELVIVA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE DISSOLUTION CLAIM BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S CONCESSION THAT THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE, GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SEEK ADJUDICATION OF DAMAGES, THE MOTION WOULD NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF ANY CAUSE OF ACTION, AND, THEREFORE, THE MOTION IS NOT A PROPER ONE UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437C(F)(1). IN ANY EVENT, THE MOTION WOULD NOT ENTIRELY RESOLVE THE CONTRACT CLAIM, BECAUSE, AS PLAINTIFF ACKNOWLEDGES, IT SEEKS ADJUCIATION OF ONLY ONE ISSUE EVEN THOUGH THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES MANY WAYS IN WHICH THE CONTRACT WAS BREACHED. AS TO THE BREACH OF DUTY CLAIM, THE MOTION FAILS FOR THE ADDITIONAL REASON THAT PLAINTIFF RELIES ON DEFENDANT HAVING THE BURDEN OF PROOF TO REBUT PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS, BUT, EVEN ASSUMING THAT IS CORRECT, PLAINTIFF CANNOT PREVAIL ON THIS MOTION WITHOUT PRESENTING ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHIING THAT DEFENDANT CANNOT MAKE SUCH A SHOWING; THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT CARRIED ITS INTITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. OPPOSING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED (504)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions
Off Calendar Apr-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Webb Legal Group (415-277-7200) for Plaintiff/Moving Party TRAVELVIVA LLC
Colby N. Keller, Esq. of Keller, Weber & Dobrott (949-553-0450) appearing by CourtCall for Defendants/Opposing Parties ALL CONTINENTS TRAVEL, INC. DBA PICASSO TRAVEL, et al.

RULING - A R G U E D: PLAINTIFF TRAVELVIVA, LLC'S Motion to Compel Depositions is granted. Requests for sanctions by both parties is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Rebecca L. Woodson; Clerk L. Fong; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Plaintiff Travelviva, Llc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Travelviva, LLC's Motion for Summary Adjudication.
No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the dissolution claim based on plaintiff's concession that there is a triable issue of fact. The motion is denied as to the other causes of action because, given that plaintiff does not seek adjudication of damages, the motion would not completely dispose of any cause of action, and, therefore, the motion is not a proper one under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(f)(1). In any event, the motion would not entirely resolve the contract claim, because, as plaintiff acknowledges, it seeks adjuciation of only one issue even though the complaint alleges many ways in which the contract was breached. As to the breach of duty claim, the motion fails for the additional reason that plaintiff relies on defendant having the burden of proof to rebut plaintiff's allegations, but, even assuming that is correct, plaintiff cannot prevail on this motion without presenting admissible evidence establishiing that defendant cannot make such a showing; therefore, plaintiff has not carried its intitial burden of production. Opposing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (504)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-05-2012 Set For Trial On Apr-29-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM WEBB, TEL. NO. 277-7200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALL CONTINENTS TRAVEL, INC. DBA PICASSO TRAVEL, RAINER KLEE, HUSEYIN OZYURTCU AND EREN AKSOY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the second and third causes of action and sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. Regarding the second cause of action, Plaintiff has properly pled a cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing that sounds in contract. (See Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 683-684.) Regarding the third cause of action, promissory estoppel is properly pled as an alternative to the breach of contract cause of action. Regarding the fourth cause of action, without more, a breach of contract cannot be a predicate for UCL liability as a matter of law. (Puentes v. Wells Fargo Home Mortg., Inc. (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 638, 645.) Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead facts showing how Defendants' actions were unlawful, unfair or fraudulent in addition to being a breach of contract. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/EG
